artifact_types.description: The project's primary artifact today: decision-log entries, design documents, prior-art synthesis, contribution guides. Files at the repo root and one level deep.
artifact_types.name: Markdown design notes
artifact_types.rationale: Markdown is the right substrate for design exploration: human- readable, diffable, renderable on GitHub, lintable.

artifact_types.description: Python source for the ontology build / validate / audit tools. Lives under tooling/src/ and tooling/tests/. Per D008 the Python gates land before any .py file did.
artifact_types.name: Python tooling
artifact_types.rationale: Python is the right substrate for the schema + build tools: pydantic v2 has the type system we need, the toolchain (pylint, mypy --strict, pytest with branch cov) is mature.

decisions.name: Treat hwdesign/cpu as exploratory blue-sky
decisions.summary: The directory is for prototype/ideation work on CPU architecture, not for shipping an artifact. Lighter process than CD-first deliverable projects: no Makefile-level build loop, no deploy target, no directory skeleton predicted in advance. Review discipline still applies on functional commits. If a concrete prototype matures into something downstream consumers depend on, spawn a new repo CD-first per the principles-transfer rule.

decisions.name: Design lens: AI writes the assembly
decisions.summary: The exploration is framed by the question: what does an ISA look like when the code generator is AI rather than a human or a classical compiler? Buried hardware ideas (Itanium EPIC, Cell BE, TRIPS, Mill) failed because compilers could not extract the parallelism the ISA exposed. AI code generators reasoning at whole-program scope relax that constraint and make those ideas worth revisiting. This is the load-bearing axiom of the project.

decisions.name: AI-compiler pipeline is co-equal to the ISA
decisions.summary: The ISA spec and the AI code-generation pipeline that targets it are designed together. Pipeline transitions are tracked via the audit DAG; provenance for the final asm output is therefore inspectable. Designed-in-isolation ISA risks repeating the EPIC/Cell/TRIPS failure mode at the AI layer.

decisions.name: Publish as a public GitHub repository
decisions.summary: Publish ai-targeted-isa publicly to invite outside collaboration. Ed Hodapp BDFL on design; collaborators contribute via GitHub with DCO sign-off. Deliberately not the single-author / no-contributions posture used in some sibling projects — the explicit aim is to surface ideas for critique by people with real Itanium / Cell / TRIPS / Mill / CHERI background.

decisions.name: License under Apache 2.0 (not MIT)
decisions.summary: Apache License 2.0 chosen for its explicit patent grant between contributors — important in CPU-architecture territory where ideas can be patented and multiple people may co-design features. Standard in adjacent ecosystems (RISC-V specs, LLVM, MLIR). Split LICENSE + COPYRIGHT layout adapted from Ed's sibling-project convention to a contribution-welcoming posture.

decisions.name: AI-compiler pipeline IS the project's CD pipeline
decisions.summary: The multi-stage AI code-generation pipeline (D003) and the project's CD pipeline are not two systems but one system at two scales. Today's markdown-only CI is the embryonic pipeline; the eventual asm-emitting pipeline is the same shape with more stages. Consequence: CD-first applies per artifact type — gates land before any artifact of a new type. No exceptions.

decisions.name: Adopt iomoments YAML+pydantic+audit ontology pattern
decisions.summary: Adopt the audit-DAG pattern proven in ~/iomoments/tooling/: hand-edited YAML, pydantic validation, content-hash-gated JSON snapshot, separate audit binary, status lifecycle, refs resolved against the repo. Schema entities re-derived for ai-targeted-isa's domain; type primitives match iomoments so cross-project audit unification stays viable later.

decisions.name: CD-first plan for the Python tooling artifact type
decisions.summary: D007 introduces Python as the second artifact type. Per D006 the Python gates (flake8, pylint, mypy --strict, pytest with branch coverage) land before any .py file. Six-step installation: pyproject.toml + deps, venv, pre-commit hook extension, CI workflow extension, deliberate-break verification cycle, then first real .py file. Steps 5 and 6 are deliberately not collapsible.

decisions.name: Pydantic schema for the ai-targeted-isa ontology (v1)
decisions.summary: Specify the v1 schema content: type primitives (SafeId, ShortName, Description, matching iomoments), two status lifecycles (RequirementStatus and DecisionStatus), six top-level entity collections (decisions, design_principles, isa_features, memory_mechanisms, pipeline_stages, artifact_types), and the ref grammar. Audit tool deferred to D010.

design_principles.description: The code generator targeting the ai-targeted-isa is AI reasoning at whole-program scope, not a classical optimizing compiler or a human asm writer. ISA design choices that assume a heroic compiler (predication, exposed pipeline, block-atomic dataflow, software-managed memory hierarchy) become viable under this assumption.
design_principles.name: AI writes the assembly
design_principles.rationale: Established in D002. The historical ISA arc has been constrained by what compilers can extract; relaxing that constraint is the project's reason for existing.

design_principles.description: The ISA spec and the AI code-generation pipeline are designed together. Every ISA-feature proposal must also state how the code generator emits it correctly and how the audit substrate verifies the result.
design_principles.name: AI-compiler pipeline is co-equal to the ISA
design_principles.rationale: Established in D003. Designed-in-isolation ISA risks repeating the EPIC/Cell/TRIPS failure mode at the AI layer.

design_principles.description: The multi-stage AI code-generation pipeline and the project's CD pipeline are one system at two scales. Adding a new artifact type means adding a new pipeline stage — and per CD-first, the gates for that type land before any artifact of that type.
design_principles.name: AI-compiler pipeline IS the CD pipeline
design_principles.rationale: Established in D006. Naming the two as one forecloses drift into parallel provenance systems.

design_principles.description: No condition-flag side effects, no implicit memory ordering, no microcoded gotchas. Every dependency syntactically visible. AI-generated mistakes manifest as wrong instructions, never as silent reordering surprises.
design_principles.name: Explicit-over-implicit semantics
design_principles.rationale: Working task #3. Identified during the prior-art synthesis as the foundational design rule that makes AI-generated asm reviewable. No decision-log entry yet; will get one when the first ISA feature design lands and adopts this rule formally.

isa_features.description: Predicated execution as the primary control-flow primitive, with branches as last resort. Reference ARM A32's rich predication that A64 stripped because compilers underused it; AI generators can use it well.
isa_features.name: First-class predication
isa_features.rationale: Working task #4. Branch mispredict cost (~15-20 cycles) dominates short-loop performance; csel-shaped predication avoids it. Open: how predication composes with block-atomic dataflow grouping.

isa_features.description: Lift POWER bdnz / RISC-V Zcb-style counted-loop instructions to first-class. Hot loops should not need branch prediction.
isa_features.name: Structured / counted loops as primitives
isa_features.rationale: Working task #5. Counted loops are perfectly predictable; the primitive lets the front-end skip prediction entirely.

isa_features.description: Instructions name their producer instructions instead of registers; hardware fires within a block as inputs arrive; blocks commit atomically. Eliminates the rename + reorder logic that dominates modern OoO area.
isa_features.name: Block-atomic dataflow grouping (TRIPS-style)
isa_features.rationale: Working task #6. Highest-leverage / highest-risk idea per the prior-art synthesis. TRIPS' compiler problem (block formation under encoding constraints) inverts most cleanly under the AI lens. Open: block-formation may be intrinsically hard in ways AI doesn't help with — needs verification.

isa_features.description: Vector-length-agnostic SIMD encoding. Software requests a logical length; hardware reports the actual length per iteration. Avoids fixed-width SSE/AVX microarchitectural cliffs.
isa_features.name: Wide scalable SIMD (RVV / SVE shape)
isa_features.rationale: Working task #7. RVV is roughly the right shape; baseline to adopt rather than research from scratch.

isa_features.description: Hardware-checked pointer + bounds + permissions. Capabilities are unforgeable; pointer arithmetic that exceeds bounds traps.
isa_features.name: CHERI-style capabilities
isa_features.rationale: Working task #8. Memory-safety value compounds when code is generated faster than humans can audit; baseline safety substrate independent of the code-gen story.

isa_features.description: Each instruction declares the memory region(s) it reads / writes / is disjoint from. Hardware parallelizes aggressively without coherence traffic; AI proves the disjointness at compile time.
isa_features.name: Per-instruction effects / region annotations
isa_features.rationale: Working task #9. Compilers can't supply this annotation reliably; AI proving non-aliasing at whole-program scope can. Major interaction with capabilities and dataflow grouping.

memory_mechanisms.description: Local store + explicit DMA, in the spirit of Cell BE SPE and CUDA shared memory, but as a general-purpose CPU tier. Compilers couldn't manage scratchpads at scale; AI can.
memory_mechanisms.name: Software-managed scratchpad as first-class ISA tier
memory_mechanisms.rationale: Working task #10. Cell's failure mode (manual DMA at scale) is exactly what AI inverts.

memory_mechanisms.description: One instruction encodes "I'm reading 4MB stride-64 starting at X" rather than letting a hardware prefetcher guess. Saves cache pollution and power.
memory_mechanisms.name: Streaming loads/stores that bypass cache
memory_mechanisms.rationale: Working task #11. The prefetcher is spending power guessing what software already knows but can't say.

memory_mechanisms.description: Lock lines into specific levels, evict explicitly, prefetch with deadline. Embedded chips have most of this; never made it to general purpose.
memory_mechanisms.name: Cache residency control as ISA
memory_mechanisms.rationale: Working task #12. AI-driven scheduling can use it on general-purpose code.

memory_mechanisms.description: A struct can be AoS in one hot loop and SoA in another. AI emits transposes / copies at the right granularity per call site; compilers can't (one type → one layout).
memory_mechanisms.name: Per-call-site data layout selection
memory_mechanisms.rationale: Working task #13. Implications for the ISA: efficient transpose primitives, scatter-gather variants, layout-aware load/store. Pipeline-stage implication: layout selection is a pipeline stage.

memory_mechanisms.description: Slice IDs, memory-controller affinities, chiplet boundaries readable and addressable. AI-scheduled code can pin working sets to slices and co-locate hot code with its memory controller.
memory_mechanisms.name: NUMA / cache-slice / chiplet placement as ISA-visible
memory_mechanisms.rationale: Working task #14. Modern cores have a topology the ISA pretends doesn't exist; runtime APIs (numactl, sched_setaffinity) work around the gap.

memory_mechanisms.description: Tell the CPU "don't speculate this — 50/50 data-dependent" or "speculate hard, 99.9% taken" based on actual program semantics. Replaces weak gcc __builtin_expect / ARM hint bits.
memory_mechanisms.name: Per-branch speculation budgets
memory_mechanisms.rationale: Working task #15. CPU is guessing because the ISA gave it no other choice; AI can supply the better signal.

pipeline_stages.description: Conversation between Ed and an AI assistant produces a reviewable design intent statement — in conversation, in an issue, or as a draft markdown section.
pipeline_stages.name: Intent capture
pipeline_stages.rationale: First stage; everything downstream derives from intent. Today intent is conversational free-form prose; structured intent schema is an open question (pipeline_design.md).

pipeline_stages.description: Draft markdown design notes (decision-log entries, design docs, prior-art entries) from intent + prior art + decisions.
pipeline_stages.name: Markdown synthesis
pipeline_stages.rationale: Where most design content lives today.

pipeline_stages.description: markdownlint, flake8, pylint, mypy, pytest run via the pre-commit hook before any commit lands locally.
pipeline_stages.name: Local gates (pre-commit)
pipeline_stages.rationale: Surface lint and quality failures locally instead of in CI.

pipeline_stages.description: GitHub Actions runs the same gates as the local hook plus lychee link checks and the build-tool sync check. Catches commits that bypassed local hooks.
pipeline_stages.name: CI gates
pipeline_stages.rationale: Independent verification at the repo level.

pipeline_stages.description: Once gates pass and the commit lands on main, the artifact joins the project's audited corpus. Git history records the promotion; the ontology DAG records provenance once D010 lands and the audit tool runs.
pipeline_stages.name: Promotion to audited corpus
pipeline_stages.rationale: Marks the boundary between candidate and canonical.
